---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

```{=html}
<section class="page-banner">
  <div style="max-width:1000px;margin:0 auto;">
    <h1>About HealthScope</h1>
    <p>Health facility intelligence &mdash; beyond static inventories</p>
  </div>
</section>
```

## Mission

**HealthScope** is an open-access health facility intelligence portal designed to move beyond static facility inventories toward dynamic, demand-driven health access analytics across malaria-endemic countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. Developed from data extracted directly from Ministries of Health, HealthScope transforms raw registry data into actionable intelligence.

The portal reveals not just *where* health facilities exist, but whether populations can realistically access them, what services they offer, and how this landscape has changed over time.

## The Gap We Fill

Existing platforms such as the WHO AFRO Health Facility Portal and the Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX) document facility locations but provide no:

- **Population-demand modelling** -- no reachability analysis
- **Temporal facility tracking** -- no understanding of change over time
- **Service-level intelligence** -- no mapping of what services exist where
- **Equity analysis at scale** -- no population-weighted access metrics

HealthScope fills all four gaps simultaneously within a lightweight, open-source, zero-server infrastructure.

## Five Intelligence Layers

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(280px,1fr));gap:1.25rem;margin:2rem 0;">

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid var(--map-red,#c9a227);">
    <h3 style="color:var(--map-red,#c9a227);margin-top:0;">1. Facility Registry Lake</h3>
    <p>Standardized facility data from MoH sources across 6+ SSA countries. Multi-country harmonized schema; direct MoH sourcing.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid #2563eb;">
    <h3 style="color:#2563eb;margin-top:0;">2. Temporal Tracking</h3>
    <p>Animated timeline of facility openings, service expansions, and closures. No existing open platform tracks facility change over time.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid #059669;">
    <h3 style="color:#059669;margin-top:0;">3. Demand-Side Access</h3>
    <p>Population catchment zones using road networks; travel-time isochrones. WHO AFRO and HDX show location only &mdash; no reachability.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid #f59e0b;">
    <h3 style="color:#f59e0b;margin-top:0;">4. Service Gap Intelligence</h3>
    <p>Which services exist where. Heatmaps of malaria service deserts, lab coverage gaps, and emergency preparedness blind spots.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="card-modern" style="border-left:4px solid #06b6d4;">
    <h3 style="color:#06b6d4;margin-top:0;">5. Equity & Vulnerability Lens</h3>
    <p>Population-weighted access scores; rural/urban parity metrics by district. Exposing inequities masked by aggregate counts.</p>
  </div>

</div>
```

## Technical Architecture

HealthScope is built on a **zero-server, open-source stack** that is free to operate, reproducible, and scalable without infrastructure investment.

```{=html}
<div style="overflow-x:auto;margin:2rem 0;">
  <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.9rem;background:#fff;border-radius:10px;overflow:hidden;box-shadow:var(--map-shadow);border:1px solid var(--map-border,#e2e8f0);">
    <thead><tr style="background:var(--map-navy,#3d3d3d);color:#fff;">
      <th style="padding:.75rem 1rem;text-align:left;">Layer</th>
      <th style="padding:.75rem 1rem;text-align:left;">Technology</th>
      <th style="padding:.75rem 1rem;text-align:left;">Role</th>
    </tr></thead>
    <tbody>
      <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--map-border);"><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;font-weight:600;">Data Extraction</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">R (httr2, rvest)</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Scrape / download from MoH registries</td></tr>
      <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--map-border);background:rgba(0,0,0,.01);"><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;font-weight:600;">ETL Pipeline</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">R (tidyverse, sf, lubridate)</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Standardize, clean, validate, flag quality</td></tr>
      <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--map-border);"><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;font-weight:600;">Storage</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">CSV + GeoParquet + DuckDB</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Spatial data lake; fast in-process queries</td></tr>
      <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--map-border);background:rgba(0,0,0,.01);"><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;font-weight:600;">Spatial Analysis</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">R (sf, osrm, WorldPop)</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Catchment zones, access scoring</td></tr>
      <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--map-border);"><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;font-weight:600;">Portal Frontend</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Quarto + Leaflet + Chart.js</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Interactive maps, dashboards, reports</td></tr>
      <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid var(--map-border);background:rgba(0,0,0,.01);"><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;font-weight:600;">Hosting</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">GitHub Pages</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Free static hosting; auto-deploy on push</td></tr>
      <tr><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;font-weight:600;">Open Data</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Zenodo / Hugging Face</td><td style="padding:.6rem 1rem;">Versioned, DOI-assigned releases</td></tr>
    </tbody>
  </table>
</div>
```

## ETL Pipeline (v2)

The HealthScope ETL pipeline processes raw MoH data through 7 validated steps:

1. **`read_country_data()`** -- reads CSV/XLSX with encoding handling
2. **`standardize_columns()`** -- maps raw columns to shared 23-field schema
3. **`clean_coordinates()`** -- coerces to numeric; flags missing/implausible GPS
4. **`clean_dates()`** -- parses Stata-format and ISO 8601 dates
5. **`enforce_schema_types()`** -- coerces all fields to correct types
6. **`flag_data_quality()`** -- assigns high/medium/low/unknown per record
7. **`save_standardized_data()`** -- writes CSV and per-country QA report

### Current Pipeline Status

| Country | Facilities | Source |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| Tanzania | 13,075 | MoH HFR Portal |
| Nigeria | 51,023 | NHMIS / DHIS2 |
| Uganda | 8,512 | MoH DHIS2 |
| Zambia | 3,731 | MoH HRIS |
| Malawi | 1,929 | MoH CMED |
| Botswana | 1,076 | MoH Registry |
| **Total** | **78,346** | |

## Funding & Acknowledgements

```{=html}
<div class="card-dark" style="margin:2rem 0;padding:2rem;border-radius:var(--map-radius,12px);">
  <p style="margin:0;font-size:1.05rem;">
    <strong>This work is supported by the Bill &amp; Melinda Gates Foundation.</strong>
    We gratefully acknowledge the Ministries of Health across East Africa for sharing national facility data,
    and the broader open-data and open-source communities whose tools and standards make this work possible.
  </p>
</div>
```

## Team

The portal is maintained by a team of data engineers, GIS specialists, epidemiologists, and researchers at the Malaria Atlas Project East Africa Node.

[**Visit the Team Page &rarr;**](team.html) | [**View Data Sources &rarr;**](data-sources.html) | [**Read Methodology &rarr;**](methodology.html)
